evals.prompt: Design the structural elements of a 2.0m x 1.5m single-cell RC box culvert in Uganda. The culvert size was determined by Skill #31 (Culvert Hydraulic Design). The fill depth is 1.2 m. Use HA loading. The subgrade is S3 (CBR 6%) with an allowable bearing capacity of 100 kPa (from Skill #10). The fill material has a unit weight of 19 kN/m3. Using the MoWT Road Design Manual Vol 4, determine: (a) the earth pressure on the culvert with correct load factors, (b) the live load surcharge, (c) preliminary slab and wall thicknesses, (d) bearing pressure check, and (e) any escalation flags.
evals.expected_output: Earth pressure: nominal P_v = 19 x 1.2 = 22.8 kN/m2, design P_v (ULS) = 1.50 x 22.8 = 34.2 kN/m2. HA surcharge = 10 kN/m2, design surcharge (ULS) = 1.50 x 10 = 15.0 kN/m2. Preliminary slab thickness 250-350 mm, wall thickness 200-300 mm. Bearing pressure well within 100 kPa allowable. Escalation flag: detailed reinforcement design required by specialist structural engineer per BS 5400 Part 4 (as referenced by MoWT Vol 4).

assertions.text: References Skill #31 as the source of culvert size -- does NOT perform hydraulic sizing (no inlet/outlet control checks)
Earth pressure calculation uses gamma_fL = 1.50 (ULS) per MoWT Vol 4 Table 1 (value within range 1.45-1.55 acceptable due to rounding)
HA surcharge correctly identified as 10 kN/m2 per MoWT Vol 4 Section 5.8.2
Preliminary member sizes provided (slab thickness 200-400 mm range, wall thickness 200-350 mm range)
Bearing pressure check performed against 100 kPa allowable from Skill #10
Escalation flag present: detailed reinforcement design requires specialist structural engineer

evals.prompt: Design the structural elements of a 2.0m x 1.5m single-cell RC box culvert for UK conditions. The culvert size was determined by hydraulic design. The fill depth is 1.5 m. The foundation soil has phi' = 30 degrees and allowable bearing capacity of 150 kPa. Determine the appropriate loading, earth pressure approach, and note any differences from Uganda practice.
evals.expected_output: UK design uses Eurocode loading (LM1, not HA/HB) and Eurocode partial factors (gamma_G = 1.35 for permanent, gamma_Q = 1.50 for variable -- different from MoWT gamma_fL = 1.50). Earth pressure K0 = 1 - sin(30) = 0.50. Foundation design uses Eurocode 7 (not CP 2004). DMRB CD 361 provides specific requirements for buried box structures. Flag: CD 361 not available locally -- engineer review required.

assertions.text: References DMRB CD 361 for UK buried concrete box structures (not MoWT Vol 4)
Uses Eurocode loading (LM1/LM3) -- does NOT use HA/HB loading for UK context
Notes that UK uses Eurocode 7 for foundation design, not CP 2004
Flags CD 361 as unavailable locally -- knowledge-based content requiring engineer review

evals.prompt: Design the structural elements of a 900mm diameter pipe culvert in Uganda with only 400mm of fill (shallow cover). The loading is HB 45 units (national road on a major freight corridor). The subgrade has bearing capacity of 80 kPa. Using the MoWT Road Design Manual Vol 4, identify the design challenges and recommend appropriate solutions.
evals.expected_output: Shallow cover warning: 400 mm < 600 mm threshold. Concentrated wheel loads dominate rather than distributed surcharge. HB 45-unit surcharge = 20 kN/m2 (high). At shallow depth, wheel loads are not fully dispersed -- need to check concentrated load distribution through 400 mm fill. Recommend Class A bedding (concrete surround, Fm = 3.4) to provide maximum structural support. Consider increasing fill depth if possible. Flag shallow cover as requiring special analysis. Flag detailed design required by specialist.

assertions.text: Identifies shallow cover warning (400 mm < 600 mm threshold) with concentrated wheel load concern
Correctly uses HB 45-unit surcharge of 20 kN/m2 (not 10 kN/m2 which is for HA/HB 25)
Recommends Class A bedding (concrete surround) or equivalent enhanced protection for shallow cover scenario
Raises escalation flag for shallow cover requiring special analysis beyond standard surcharge approach
References bedding factor method with appropriate bedding class selection
